title: plantago coronopus subsp. commutata
intro: Plantago coronopus subsp. commutata, also known as buck's-horn plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain genus, characterized by its distinctive buck's-horn shaped leaves. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin irritations, wounds, and burns, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Plantago coronopus subsp. commutata include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its potential to aid in the healing process of wounds and burns. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions or tinctures, which can be applied topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
